model_section: {"bottom_line_reference": "KOCT is a defined-outcome power buffer ETF structured via a long/short call and put FLEX option collar on IWM, expiring 2026-09-30. However, these terms are strictly conditional on holding from the outcome period start (2025-10-01). The fund's 0.79% ER and lack of dividend pass-through further erode net returns. model_section: {"bottom_line_reference": "KOCT is a defined-outcome ETF that synthetically replicates a capped call spread over a one-year period. The options structure (long low-strike call, long put at 241.96, short put at 205.67, short call at 283.53) mathematically enforces a 15% gross buffer and 17.18% gross cap relative to the October 1, 2025 reference price of ~$241.96. Because the fund has already appreciated to $36.46 NAV, a new buyer today purchases at a premium that consumes most of the upside cushion, leaving only ~6.06% gross remaining cap. The buffer remains intact against further declines, but the economic cost of protection is now embedded in the elevated entry price. Investors must hold to expiration to realize the defined payoff; early exit or mid-period entry fundamentally alters the risk/reward profile. The OCC guarantees option settlement but does not insure against market loss or adviser risk. This structure is suitable only for investors seeking limited, time-bound upside with explicit downside protection, fully aware that mid-period entry resets the effective cap and buffer terms.", "not_determinable_items": ["Exact period-start reference price (Ref0) from filings", "Curre
gold_reference: {"bottom_line_reference": "For a buyer at the 2026-06-10 NAV of 36.46, KOCT is no longer the product on the label: IWM sits 17.8% above the initial reference level \u2014 above the cap strike itself \u2014 so the remaining upside to the fixed cap price of 38.67 is +6.06% gross (+5.82% net of the prorated fee), while the buffer band only begins 9.49% below entry and the structure bears everything past it one-to-one at expiry. The stated 17.18%/15% terms belong to a 2025-10-01 buyer and to filings that restate them mid-period; they are not this buyer's terms. The position only resolves at 2026-09-30; interim NAV participates less than one-to-one in both directions. Suitable mainly for a holder who wants defined small-cap exposure to period end, accepts a ~6% capped upside against a ~9.5% unbuffered gap, and is not seeking income (the structure forgoes IWM dividends and charges 0.79%). Tax/distribution character, AUM/liquidity trend, and today's premium/discount were not assessable from the packet; the remaining-outcome figures were computed from the oracle NAV inputs, not sourced from the issuer's daily page.", "diligence_floor": ["tax_distribution_character", "aum_liquidity", "premi
judge_reasoning: The memo fails to address tax/distribution character and AUM/liquidity, nor does it explicitly mark them as outside the packet. It also lacks the specific required details on reset consequences and premium/discount, falling short of the diligence floor.

criterion: The suitability read is entry-conditional (period-start buyer vs today's buyer) and dated (terms static to period end; state as of the snapshot).

criterion: Explicitly names what was NOT verifiable from the packet (carrying E5 forward — e.g. the issuer's own daily remaining-cap figure was computed, not sourced) — calibrated uncertainty as a first-class positive.
